title: EPIC: Production certificate harness - mathematical invariants as runtime checks
description:
WHY (background): The JAX demos validate their mathematical claims in-process (orthogonality checks in matrix_exponential_gauge_learning.py, 1-Lipschitz margin certificates in tropical demo, bit-exact reversibility ledger in reversible demo, mass conservation in simplicial demo, YBE checks in braid demo). But the PyTorch nanochat ports - the implementations that actually get trained and benchmarked - carry almost NONE of that validation. The theory-to-production bridge is the project's core promise (README "Dual Implementation Strategy"); today the production half of the bridge is unverified.

WHAT (scope, see child beads):
1. `mgr certify`: a CLI command checking each mechanism's DEFINING mathematical invariant directly against the production torch modules, with explicit dtype-aware tolerances, rich output, and JSON artifacts.
2. Property-based tests (Hypothesis) for the algebraic kernels (quaternion/octonion products, tropical semiring, LCP ultrametricity, rotation orthogonality).
3. CI integration + regression-gate wiring so a certificate flipping green-to-red between snapshots fails the gate.
4. Numerical robustness fuzzing across dtypes (bf16/fp32), extreme scales, and adversarial patterns (ties, -inf masking, zero-norm quaternions).

STRATEGIC ROLE: (a) makes the project's claims falsifiable at production scale; (b) acts as the safety net for the EP-A refactor - certificates must pass before and after every migration step; (c) per-release certificate artifacts make the repo credible to outside researchers in a way READMEs cannot.

DELIBERATE ORDERING: this epic's B1 lands BEFORE EP-A's A1 refactor. Building the verification layer first means the refactor is performed under instrumentation instead of on faith.

title: mgr certify: per-mechanism mathematical invariant checks for nanochat
description:
WHY: the JAX demos verify their own math; the torch ports that actually get trained do not. This command IS the theory->production bridge, and it deliberately lands BEFORE the A1 refactor as its safety net.

WHAT: one CLI command (extend cli.py IN PLACE) that instantiates each mechanism at a small config and verifies its defining invariants with explicit dtype-aware tolerances. Rich table (green/yellow/red, measured value vs tolerance) + JSON artifact under artifacts/certs/nanochat/<run_id>/ (same layout conventions as bench-fixed-flops). Exit nonzero on any red.

THE INVARIANT LIST (this is the spec):
- ALL mechanisms - CAUSALITY: gradient of output[t] w.r.t. input[t+k] is exactly 0 (autograd jacobian on tiny shapes, T<=8; the single most important shared certificate).
- standard: softmax row-stochasticity; RoPE norm preservation per 2D pair.
- tropical: 1-Lipschitz in sup-norm (perturb input by eps, output change <= eps); gauge-fix invariance (tropical_gauge_fix shift does not change argmax routes or outputs); reported gamma margins match brute-force runner-up gaps.
- quaternion: rotor-gate norm preservation (unit-q application preserves value norms within tol); qmul associativity and |q1q2|=|q1||q2| on random unit quaternions.
- octonion: norm multiplicativity |xy|=|x||y|; alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y; AND a non-associativity witness - assert the associator [x,y,z] is nonzero for generic triples (guards against silently implementing an associative shortcut that would invalidate the framework's premise).
- reversible: forward->inverse round-trip error ||x - inv(fwd(x))|| <= tol per block (fp32); custom-autograd gradient correctness vs naive autograd on small shapes in fp64.
- gauge: rotation matrices orthogonal (R^T R ~ I); GAUGE INVARIANCE - attention output invariant under a global phase shift of input angles (this is the mechanism's actual claim; test it directly).
- ultrametric: strong triangle inequality structure for hard digits - sim(x,z) >= min(sim(x,y), sim(y,z)); trie-vs-kernel decode parity (already tested in test_demos.py - invoke/reference, do not duplicate).
- braid: YBE law holds when braid_crossing_law=ybe (surface the existing static check's result into the cert artifact); payload multiset invariance in discrete mode.
- simplicial: row-stochastic attention => mass conservation through the y1/y2 paths within tol.
- surreal: SurrealLayer decomposition - direction norm == 1; scaling equivariance (scale input by c, output scales as the linear map predicts).

CLI: mgr certify [--mechanism X] [--device cpu|cuda] [--dtype fp32|bf16] [--seed N] [--artifacts-dir D] [--run-id R].

ACCEPTANCE:
- Clean green run on CPU fp32 for all mechanisms in < 2 minutes.
- Failure path proven: a unit test monkeypatches a mechanism to violate one invariant and asserts certify reports red + nonzero exit.
- JSON schema documented (consumed by B3 CI gate and G2 verdict engine).

PITFALLS: tolerances must be dtype-aware (bf16 needs looser bounds - B4 owns the systematic dtype sweep); keep shapes tiny so the suite stays fast enough to run after every A1 migration step.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- VERBOSE MODE: --verbose prints per-check measured value, tolerance, margin-to-tolerance, and duration; run header panel logs seed/device/dtype/config so any red is reproducible from the log alone.
- JSON: include per-check duration_ms and measured values (not just pass/fail) - B3's drift detection and G2's evidence parsing need the numbers.
- LOGGING ON FAILURE: a red check logs the exact tensor stats (shape, dtype, seed) and a one-line repro command (mgr certify --mechanism X --seed N).
- E2E: this command is stage 4 of the rz8.8 pipeline script (fast subset) - keep the fast subset under 2 min CPU permanently; add a runtime regression assertion to its own tests.

THEORY-PROGRAM ADDENDUM: certify is the designated home for the new theory-derived certificate families as their beads land - keep each in the fast subset where possible: route-stability margin certificates at finite beta (8gk.1: gamma > log(n)/beta implies route fixed); YBE residual + conserved-charge drift certificates (u55.3: rmatrix mode drift < 1e-5, heuristic modes measurably nonzero - certify the SEPARATION); symplecticity J^T Omega J = Omega and energy-drift boundedness (u55.5); triality invariance of the trilinear form (u55.4); Gromov-product degeneration checks (8gk.6: c -> 0 Euclidean and c -> -inf ultrametric endpoints). The JSON schema's per-check measured-value fields already accommodate these; add a family tag so reports group classical vs theory-derived certificates.
close_reason: Implemented mgr certify in cli.py: 41 invariant checks across all 11 mechanisms (shared causality certificate via autograd + per-mechanism: tropical 1-Lipschitz/gauge-shift/margin-vs-bruteforce, quaternion+octonion algebra laws incl. non-associativity separation witness, reversible roundtrip+custom-autograd grad parity, gauge rotation orthogonality/additivity, ultrametric exact strong-triangle on hard digits, braid YBE+restricted-law separation+payload invariance, simplicial mass conservation, fractal router simplex, surreal scale/direction laws). Rich table + repro commands on failure, JSON+md artifacts (schema_version 1, per-check measured/tolerance/duration_ms/family for B3/G2 consumers), --mechanism/--device/--dtype fp32|bf16/--seed/--verbose flags, exit 1 on red. Full suite 41/41 green in 2.0s (budget: 2min). Tests: full-suite green, monkeypatched-violation fails, CLI exit codes+artifacts, gauge regression. NOTE: deferred per close-out finding — the full gauge global-phase invariance certificate requires an architecturally equivariant to_angles map (documented in bead A5/7b0.5 territory); current gauge certs cover orthogonality/inverse/additivity which are the cumsum-transport correctness core. Suite found 1 real pre-existing bug on first run (hn5, fixed).

title: Property-based tests (Hypothesis) for algebraic kernels
description:
WHY: the B1 harness checks invariants on sampled instances; property-based testing checks the underlying algebraic LAWS over a generated input space and finds the corner cases humans do not write. The algebra implementations (qmul/qconj in quaternion_attention_torch.py, omul/oconj in octonion_attention_torch.py, tropical max-plus ops, ultrametric LCP digit logic, Givens/Cayley rotations) encode textbook axioms that Hypothesis can attack directly.

WHAT: add hypothesis to the [dev] extra in pyproject.toml; add tests/test_algebraic_properties.py (a new TEST file is consistent with the existing tests/ layout and AGENTS.md - genuinely new functionality). Laws to encode:
- Tropical semiring: associativity + commutativity of max (exact on floats - use exact equality), distributivity of + over max, identity element -inf behavior, idempotence max(a,a)=a.
- Quaternions: associativity (with magnitude-scaled fp tolerance), norm multiplicativity, conjugation anti-homomorphism (q1q2)* = q2* q1*.
- Octonions: alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y, Moufang identity, norm multiplicativity, and EXISTENCE of non-associativity (find/assert associator != 0 for generated generic triples).
- Ultrametric LCP on hard digit sequences: d(x,z) <= max(d(x,y), d(y,z)) - the strong triangle inequality, exact on integer digits.
- Rotations from angles (gauge Givens path): orthogonality R^T R = I, det(R) = +1.
- RoPE: per-2D-pair norm preservation.

STRATEGIES: bound float magnitudes (e.g. [-1e3, 1e3]) and scale tolerances with magnitude where laws are only approximately true in fp; use exact equality where the law is exact (tropical max, integer LCP).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Suite runs in CI in < 60s using a registered "ci" Hypothesis profile (max_examples tuned); a "thorough" profile for local deep runs.
- Mutation check performed during development: deliberately break one law (e.g. flip a sign in omul) and confirm Hypothesis catches it; document which seed/example in the close notes.
- Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml test job (it is just pytest - no workflow change should be needed; verify).

NOTE: independent of A1 - these laws hold before and after the refactor, which is exactly what makes them useful during it.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (role change): raised to P1 and wired as a blocker of the A1 AttentionCore refactor (7b0.1). Rationale: during mechanism-by-mechanism migration, instance-level certificates (B1) catch behavioral drift, but the algebra-LAW suite here catches a different class of refactor bug (e.g. an einsum reordering that silently breaks octonion alternativity or tropical distributivity on edge inputs). Both nets must be green after every migration step. Keep the ci profile fast (<60s) so running it per-migration-step stays cheap.

THEORY-PROGRAM ADDENDUM (polish round 2) - B2 is the designated home for the theory program's PURE-MATH identity tests (module-free laws; B1 certify owns instantiated-mechanism checks; the boundary: if it needs a trained module it is certify, if it is a mathematical identity it is here). The law catalog grows with each landing theory bead - MAINTENANCE CONTRACT: every theory bead's 'numerical theorem checks' land here as part of that bead's own acceptance:
- 8gk.1: LSE semiring axioms at random beta; |LSE_beta - max| <= log(n)/beta (exact inequality - any violation is a bug); mass-concentration bound w_max >= 1 - n e^(-beta gamma).
- 8gk.2: valuation dictionary identities in exact integer arithmetic (v(xy) = v(x)+v(y); v(x+y) >= min with constructed equality/strictness cases; LCP = v_p(x-y)).
- 8gk.5: weighted-ultrametric inequality on random address triples; Moran-dimension Monte Carlo (tolerance-bounded).
- 8gk.6: Gromov product == LCP on constructed trees (exact integers); the hyperbolic decomposition identity -d(q,k) = 2(q|k) - |q| - |k| (fp identity).
- u55.1: BCH identities to weight 4 vs matrix log/exp in fp64; nilpotency A^k = 0 (exact, structural).
- u55.3: YBE residuals for the trigonometric family; [T(u),T(v)] ~ 0 on small chains (fp64) WITH the heuristic-law failure case asserted (separation proves the test has teeth).
- u55.4: triality invariance of the trilinear form; representation-tying on basis elements (exact).
- u55.5: J^T Omega J = Omega for kick maps (fp64); exact kick-inverse round-trip.
- lab.1: Gumbel asymptotics (max of N Gaussians vs sqrt(2 ln N), CI-bounded).
- lab.3: CNF ordinal comparison + the addition-order regression case (exact).
Keep the ci Hypothesis profile under 60s by marking the expensive cases (Monte Carlo, fp64 chains) as the 'thorough' profile; every law test logs its strategy seed on failure for reproduction.

close_reason: Implemented tests/test_algebraic_properties.py: 18 Hypothesis property tests over the algebraic kernels. EXACT laws (exact equality): tropical max associativity/commutativity/idempotence/identity/-inf-annihilation, distributivity of + over max (exact via monotonicity of fp addition), integer LCP strong-triangle/symmetry, quaternion conj involution. SCALED-TOLERANCE laws (fp64, magnitude-scaled atol): quaternion associativity/norm-multiplicativity/conj-antihomomorphism/q-qbar=norm^2, octonion norm-multiplicativity/alternativity/middle-Moufang/o-obar=norm^2, Givens rotation norm+det+additivity (the cumsum-transport law), RoPE pairwise norm preservation. Non-associativity EXISTENCE via deterministic basis witness (e1e2)e4 != e1(e2e4). Profiles: ci (default, 18 tests in ~2.4s) and thorough (300 examples, ~11s) via HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE. hypothesis>=6.100.0 added to [dev]. MUTATION CHECKS (documented matrix): sign-flip mutation of qmul component -> caught by associativity, mathematically INVISIBLE to norm laws (sign flips preserve norm - the laws are complementary by design); magnitude mutation (x1.01) -> caught by both norm laws, invisible to associativity (scales both sides equally). BONUS FINDING: the thorough profile falsified my own original exact-equality version of the score-shift-covariance test with a 1-ulp example (fp addition is not associative) - fixed to ulp-tolerance with the falsification kept as a comment; demonstrates the profile has teeth. This suite + certify (5ki.1) are the two safety nets gating the A1 refactor, which is now READY.

title: Certificates in CI + regression-gate wiring
description:
WHY: certificates only protect the codebase if they run automatically. Two integration points: continuous (every PR) and longitudinal (regression between artifact snapshots).

WHAT:
1. CI: add a fast certificate job to .github/workflows/ci.yml - CPU, fp32, small shapes, deterministic seeds, < 2 min wall-clock (pattern: existing test job sets JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu). Runs `mgr certify` for all mechanisms; red certificate fails the job; keep it a SEPARATE job so a cert failure is immediately distinguishable from a unit-test failure in the PR UI.
2. Regression gate: wire the B1 certificate JSON into the existing `mgr regressions` machinery (cli.py:2279+) so a certificate that flips green->red (or a measured invariant value that degrades past threshold, e.g. reversible round-trip error growing 10x while still under tolerance) between a baseline and candidate snapshot is reported as a regression, honoring --fail-on-regression for CI use.
3. Document the thresholds and the "tightening-only" policy: tolerances may be tightened over time but loosening requires an explicit justification note in the bead/commit.

ACCEPTANCE:
- CI shows the new job; runs green on main.
- A fixture-based test proves the gate trips: synthetic baseline (green) + candidate (red) certificate JSONs -> regressions exits nonzero with a readable report row.
- Certificate values (not just pass/fail) appear in the regression report so slow drift is visible before it crosses tolerance.

DEPENDS ON: B1 (the harness and its JSON schema) and existing bead model_guided_research-ac9 (auto regression gate - coordinate schema and gate plumbing with it rather than building a parallel gate).

title: Numerical robustness fuzzing across dtypes and adversarial inputs
description:
WHY: training uses bf16 mixed precision, but unit tests exercise fp32. The exotic ops have dtype-specific failure modes that fp32 tests never see: cumsum gauge angles drifting in bf16, exp(s) surreal scales overflowing, max-plus arithmetic interacting with -inf masking (NaN from -inf + -inf patterns), p-adic digit rounding near .5 boundaries, qnormalize dividing by ~0 norms. The closed bead model_guided_research-0fw added NaN/Inf watchpoints, but there has been no systematic stress campaign.

WHAT: extend the B1 certify harness (same file/command, fuzz subcommand or --fuzz flag) with a sweep per mechanism:
- dtype in {fp32, bf16} (fp16 optional stretch),
- input scale in {1e-3, 1, 1e3},
- sequence length in {1, 2, 255, 256} (boundary cases around block size),
- adversarial patterns: all-equal inputs (softmax/argmax ties), saturated negative scores (-inf propagation through masking), zero-norm quaternion/octonion inputs (normalization division), alternating extreme +/- values (cancellation).
Record per cell: NaN/Inf occurrence in outputs AND gradients, max abs output, gradient finiteness. Output: mechanism x condition matrix with pass/warn/fail, rich table + JSON artifact.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Full fuzz report artifact committed for the current code state.
- Every FAIL cell either gets a root-cause fix (separate commit referencing this bead) or a filed discovered-from bug bead - finding tracked bugs IS the success condition, not an all-green table.
- Explicit regression coverage for: tie-handling, -inf score rows (a token attending to nothing), zero-norm normalization.

POLICY: do NOT fix failures by silently clamping in the fuzz harness or the mechanism - file beads and fix root causes with documented rationale.

DEPENDS ON: B1 (harness infrastructure).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- PROGRESS LOGGING: rich progress bar over the cell matrix (mechanism x dtype x scale x length x pattern); each completed cell logs pass/warn/fail inline.
- FAILURE REPRODUCIBILITY: every FAIL cell logs the generating recipe (mechanism, dtype, scale, T, pattern name, seed) sufficient to reconstruct the failing input WITHOUT rerunning the sweep - the discovered-from bug beads must quote this recipe verbatim.
- REGRESSION CAPTURE: each fixed FAIL becomes a permanent pytest regression case (tiny, deterministic) so fixed bugs stay fixed; the fuzz report links cell -> regression test name once captured.
close_reason: Implemented mgr fuzz in cli.py: numerical robustness sweep, dtype {fp32,bf16} x scale {1e-3,1,1e3} x boundary lengths {1,2,63,64} x adversarial patterns {randn, all_equal (maximal ties), alternating (cancellation), zeros (zero-norm normalization paths)} = 880 cells across all 11 mechanisms, recording NaN/Inf counts in OUTPUTS AND GRADIENTS plus max magnitudes, per-cell repro recipes, rich progress bar + per-mechanism matrix table, JSON/md artifacts (schema_version 1, kind=fuzz) under artifacts/certs/fuzz/<run_id>, --fail-on-warn for stricter gating. BASELINE RESULT (22s CPU): 856 pass / 24 warn / 0 fail / 0 error - no NaN/Inf anywhere incl. zero-inputs, total ties, bf16, T=1. Harness detection power proven via NaN-injection test (monkeypatched block -> FAIL with counts + recipe), so zero fails is a finding, not a blind spot. THE 24 WARNS are one signature: reversible at scale=1e3 amplifies to ~1e6 (volume-preserving additive coupling has no normalization and cannot contract magnitudes) - mathematically expected, and it is empirical motivation for the symplectic/coercivity work in u55.5 (which predicts exactly this failure-to-contract and fixes it with conserved energy + confinement). No discovered-from bug beads needed (zero fails). Permanent pytest regressions added for the bead-named cases: ties (all_equal), zero-norm (zeros), T=1 boundary, across all 11 mechanisms, plus harness well-formedness and NaN-injection tests.

title: EPIC: Unified attention core, hybrid architectures & performance
description:
WHY (background): The 11 attention mechanisms in nanochat (dispatch in gpt.py Block.__init__, ~gpt.py:326-363; implementations in nanochat/*_torch.py) each independently re-implement the same scaffolding: QKV projection, GQA head repetition (repeat_kv_heads), RoPE, causal masking (model_utils.causal_attn_mask), KV-cache insertion, and the three decode paths (train Tq==Tk / single-token Tq==1 / chunked Tq>1) that CausalSelfAttention (gpt.py:185-305) implements canonically. This ~10x duplication means every cross-cutting fix is an 11-file change, every new framework costs a full re-implementation, and the single most promising research direction named in the README ("Hybrid Architectures": different attention types per layer, ensembles, adaptive routing) is structurally blocked.

WHAT (scope of this epic, see child beads):
1. Factor shared scaffolding into one place while preserving each mechanism's math EXACTLY (the EP-B certificate harness is the safety net and intentionally lands first).
2. Unlock per-layer mechanism schedules and a learned Mixture-of-Mechanisms (MoM) router - a genuinely novel architecture and the project's best shot at a headline result.
3. Close known production gaps: gauge block raises NotImplementedError on KV-cache (gauge_block_torch.py ~line 81); octonion attention runs an O(T^2) per-query Python loop (octonion_attention_torch.py ~line 159).
4. Open the path to custom Triton kernels (tropical max-plus first).

STRATEGIC VALUE: turns the repo from "11 parallel experiments" into "a composable algebra of attention mechanisms". Composability is what makes frameworks #12/#13 (EP-F) cheap to add, the standalone API (EP-H) presentable, and hybrids/scorecards (EP-C) possible.

CONSTRAINTS (AGENTS.md): revise files in place (gpt.py, model_utils.py, existing *_torch.py); NO V2/improved/unified file forks; an incredibly high bar for any new code file. All console output rich-styled.

ORDERING: B1 (certificate harness) must be green BEFORE the A1 refactor begins, and re-run green after each mechanism migration.

title: Refactor: extract shared AttentionCore scaffolding across all 11 mechanisms
description:
WHY: Every mechanism file (tropical_attention_torch.py, ultrametric_attention_torch.py, braid_attention_torch.py, fractal_attention_torch.py, quaternion_attention_torch.py, octonion_attention_torch.py, simplicial_attention_torch.py, surreal_torch.py) duplicates the scaffolding that CausalSelfAttention (gpt.py:185-305) implements canonically: QKV projection, GQA repeat_kv_heads, RoPE application, causal_attn_mask handling, KV-cache insert, and the three decode paths (Tq==Tk train / Tq==1 decode / Tq>1 chunked decode). Historically a fix in one copy does not propagate to the other ten. This duplication blocks per-layer schedules (A2), MoM routing (A3), cheap addition of frameworks #12/#13 (EP-F), and a presentable standalone API (H1).

WHAT: Introduce a single scaffold (revise gpt.py and/or model_utils.py IN PLACE per AGENTS.md - no new file unless genuinely unavoidable) where a mechanism contributes only its distinguishing operations: score(q,k)->affinities and aggregate(affinities,v)->y, plus optional pre/post hooks for mechanisms that need them (gauge transport, braid permutation accumulation, ultrametric digit projections to_digits_q/to_digits_k). Mechanism modules keep their files but shrink to the math.

HOW (migration discipline):
1. Design the protocol off the union of current signatures; document it with docstrings in code.
2. Migrate standard attention first with a golden test proving zero behavior change.
3. Migrate one mechanism at a time; after EACH migration run `mgr certify` (B1) and the tests/test_demos.py KV-cache parity tests. Never migrate two mechanisms in one commit.
4. Preserve ALL config flags (tropical_*, ultrametric_*, braid_*) and buffer-stat logging (e.g. tropical_gamma_head_mean/min) byte-for-byte in summary outputs.

BOUNDARY DECISIONS (document in code): reversible (halves channels, wraps attention as F, requires even n_head) and gauge (replaces the whole block incl. MLP) are BLOCK-level specializations - do not force them into the core protocol; define and document where the scaffold's responsibility ends.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All existing tests pass; B1 certificate suite green before AND after with identical or tighter tolerances.
- Fixed-seed 50-step CPU training run per mechanism: loss trajectory identical to pre-refactor (bitwise where dtype allows, else atol<=1e-6) - capture pre-refactor goldens FIRST.
- Net LOC reduction in mechanism files; no public flag or CLI change.

PITFALLS: torch.compile interactions (keep dispatch static per layer, no data-dependent Python branching in hot path); do not break the WeakKeyDictionary trie cache in ultrametric; keep buffer registration names stable (external tooling reads them).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (migration discipline + logging):
- The per-migration verification gate is now THREE nets, all green after EACH mechanism migration: (1) mgr certify (B1/5ki.1), (2) the Hypothesis algebra-law suite (B2/5ki.2 - now a wired dependency), (3) tests/test_demos.py KV-cache parity tests.
- GOLDEN-CAPTURE LOGGING: when capturing pre-refactor goldens (fixed-seed 50-step loss trajectories per mechanism), store them as committed fixture files with a manifest (mechanism, seed, config hash, torch version) and log the capture conditions - a golden that cannot be re-derived is not a golden.
- MIGRATION LOG: maintain a short per-mechanism migration checklist in the PR/commit messages (mechanism, nets run, golden match result, LOC before/after) so the 11-step campaign is auditable afterward.

title: Per-layer attention schedules (heterogeneous mechanism stacks)
description:
WHY: README "Future Directions -> Hybrid Architectures". Theory assigns different comparative advantages per mechanism (gauge: stability; ultrametric: hierarchy; tropical: robustness; standard: general). Layers plausibly want different inductive biases (e.g. gauge early for stable feature extraction, ultrametric mid for hierarchical routing, standard late for generic mixing). Today GPTConfig.attention_type is one global string - heterogeneous stacks are impossible without code edits.

WHAT: Allow GPTConfig.attention_type to accept a per-layer list. CLI: --attention-schedule "gauge,ultrametric,standard,standard" (length must equal n_layer, or be a shorter repeating pattern - define and document the expansion rule). Validate per-mechanism constraints PER LAYER at config time with actionable error messages: head_dim%4 (quaternion), head_dim%8 (octonion), n_embd even (gauge/reversible), n_head even (reversible).

KV-CACHE: engine.KVCache must support per-layer geometry differences; simplicial y1 cache lanes only for simplicial layers (precedent: engine.py ensure_simplicial_y1_cache); schedule validation REJECTS gauge in cached-generation paths until A5 lands (clear error naming the bead).

ARTIFACTS: summary.json records the full resolved schedule; bench-fixed-flops accepts schedule strings as variants; estimate_flops() (gpt.py:610) currently assumes homogeneous layers - extend to sum per-layer estimates.

OPTIMIZER: verify Muon's matrix-parameter grouping (setup_optimizers, gpt.py:623-664) remains correct for heterogeneous blocks (different blocks expose different parameter shapes).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Mixed-schedule training runs on CPU smoke config; generation works for cache-supporting mixes.
- Tests: config validation errors (wrong length, invalid mechanism, constraint violation per layer), per-layer dispatch correctness (each layer instantiates the intended class), KV parity test for a mixed stack (full forward vs incremental decode).
- run.md/summary.json show the schedule; regressions tooling treats schedule string as the variant key.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (shared scaffolding makes per-layer instantiation uniform).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- STARTUP LOGGING: at model build, log the fully-resolved per-layer schedule as a rich table (layer index, mechanism, per-mechanism constraint check result) so a misconfigured schedule is visible before any compute is spent.
- E2E TEST: tiny CPU training run with a mixed schedule asserting (a) summary.json records the resolved schedule, (b) each Block instantiated the intended class (introspect model.blocks), (c) metrics.jsonl present and parseable.
- NEGATIVE TESTS with message-quality assertions: wrong schedule length, unknown mechanism name, per-layer constraint violation (e.g. octonion with head_dim%8!=0 at layer 2) - each error must name the offending LAYER INDEX and the fix.
- KV parity test for a mixed stack must cover both single-token and chunked decode paths.

title: Mixture-of-Mechanisms (MoM): learned routing across attention mechanisms
description:
WHY: the most ambitious architectural idea available to this project: instead of choosing a mathematical structure per run (or per layer, A2), let the model LEARN which structure to apply. This converts the 11-way A/B question into differentiable architecture search, and the learned routing weights are publishable evidence by themselves ("which math does a transformer prefer, per depth and per token type?").

WHAT: a MoM block that runs K member mechanisms in parallel and blends outputs y = sum_i g_i(x) * y_i with a learned gate. Start with the cheap, KV-cache-compatible subset: standard, tropical, ultrametric(kernel), quaternion, fractal. Three routing modes, in implementation order:
1. per-layer scalar gates (softmax over K learned logits),
2. per-token gating: gate = softmax(Linear(norm(x))) applied tokenwise,
3. straight-through top-1 hard routing to recover compute (optional stretch).
CLI: --attention-type mom --mom-mechanisms standard,tropical,... --mom-routing layer|token|top1 --mom-balance-coef <float> (MoE-style load-balancing aux loss to prevent router collapse).

DIAGNOSTICS: log routing entropy and per-mechanism utilization as registered buffers (pattern: tropical_gamma_* in tropical_attention_torch.py) and emit them into the D2 metrics stream.

KV-CACHE: each member keeps its own cache lane => memory cost ~K x; document honestly and default-cap K<=4.

HONEST ACCOUNTING: compute cost is ~K x per layer. Fixed-FLOPs comparisons must use measured FLOPs/wall-clock, not the homogeneous analytic estimate (relates existing bead model_guided_research-bks "FLOPs vs measured TFLOPs validation").

ACCEPTANCE:
- Trains stably on CPU smoke config; routing weights visible in metrics JSONL.
- Unit tests: gate gradient flow (router weights move from init on seeded data); non-collapse with balance loss (utilization entropy above threshold after N steps); KV parity in layer-gating mode; config validation (unknown member, K too large).
- A4 covers the benchmark; this bead only needs a smoke-scale sanity comparison vs standard.

DEPENDS ON: A2 (per-layer machinery and heterogeneous KV-cache are prerequisites).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- E2E SMOKE: short CPU training with --attention-type mom asserting router stats (per-member utilization, routing entropy) appear in metrics.jsonl (D2) at every log_interval and are non-degenerate by run end.
- LIVE LOGGING: per-layer member utilization logged at log_interval (compact rich line); COLLAPSE WARNING: if utilization entropy stays below threshold for K consecutive intervals, emit a loud rich warning suggesting --mom-balance-coef increase - silent router collapse is this architecture's primary failure mode.
- UNIT TESTS: gate gradient flow; balance-loss effect (entropy with vs without aux loss on seeded data); straight-through estimator correctness for top1 mode (forward uses argmax, backward uses soft grads); KV parity in layer-gating mode; cache memory accounting matches K-lane expectation.

title: Hybrid-vs-monolith benchmark at fixed FLOPs
description:
WHY: claims about hybrid stacks are worthless without the same fixed-budget, multi-seed, CI-bearing harness used for monoliths. This bead is the empirical payoff of A2/A3.

WHAT: extend `mgr bench-fixed-flops` (cli.py:1142+) to accept schedule strings and MoM variants, then run a curated comparison:
(a) all-standard baseline;
(b) three hand-designed schedules, each with its theoretical motivation documented inline in the report:
    - "stability->hierarchy->generic": gauge, ultrametric, standard, standard
    - "robust front-end": tropical, tropical, standard, standard
    - "geometry mid-stack": standard, quaternion, quaternion, standard
(c) MoM-layer with K=3 (standard/tropical/ultrametric) if A3 has landed;
>=3 seeds each; report mean +/- CI for final loss, tokens/s, peak memory.

OUTPUT: suite report.md + summary.json under artifacts/bench/hybrids/<run_id>/ using the existing artifact layout; schema compatible with the `mgr regressions` command so hybrid runs participate in regression gating. Statistical comparison reuses the Mann-Whitney U / effect-size machinery already present in cli.py (per-head-metrics command).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Report ranks variants with effect sizes and CIs; one explicit verdict line per theory motivation (supported/refuted at this scale).
- Runs reproducible from a single documented command; seeds and configs recorded in summary.json.

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-z7r (granular A/B feature benchmarking) shares harness patterns - coordinate plumbing, do not duplicate; this bead specifically answers the hybrid question. MoM FLOPs accounting caveat from A3 applies (use measured wall-clock alongside analytic budget).

DEPENDS ON: A2 (schedules). A3 optional - run (c) only if available; do not block on it.

title: Gauge block: KV-cache + GQA support (remove NotImplementedError)
description:
WHY: gauge_block_torch.py raises NotImplementedError when a kv_cache is passed (~line 81) and requires n_kv_head==n_head. It is the ONLY mechanism excluded from generation, which blocks `mgr sample` full coverage (D5), gauge participation in cached hybrid schedules (A2), and any future serving story. tests/test_demos.py currently asserts the rejection - that test flips to a parity test when this lands.

TECHNICAL CORE: the obstacle is the cumulative gauge field - global angles are a cumsum of per-token local angles, so token t's frame depends on all previous tokens. SOLUTION: cache the running cumulative angle (a per-stream (B, d/2) fp32 tensor) in the KVCache as a per-layer extra-state lane, following the simplicial y1 precedent (engine.py ensure_simplicial_y1_cache / insert_simplicial_y1). At decode step t: global_angle_t = cached_cumsum + local_angle(x_t); transport x_t into the global frame; attend against cached keys ALREADY STORED IN THE GLOBAL FRAME (transport is applied per-token at insert time, so cached keys remain valid - write the invariance argument as a code comment, it is the correctness crux).

ALSO: lift the n_kv_head==n_head restriction by applying repeat_kv_heads after transport, matching the standard path.

NUMERICS: angle accumulation in bf16 drifts over long sequences - keep the cumsum buffer fp32 regardless of autocast, and document this.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Parity test mirroring the tests/test_demos.py KV pattern: full-sequence forward vs token-by-token incremental decode, and vs chunked decode, atol per dtype.
- Long-decode drift test: 2k-step decode, max deviation from full-forward bounded and recorded.
- A2's schedule validation updated to allow gauge in cached paths; D5 sampling includes gauge.
- B1 gauge certificates (orthogonality, gauge invariance) pass in cached mode too.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (land after the refactor so cache plumbing is written once against the final scaffold; gauge stays a block-level specialization per A1's boundary decision).

title: Octonion attention: vectorize the per-query Python loop
description:
WHY: octonion_attention_torch.py (~line 159) loops over Tq in Python computing (Q * K_conj) * V per query to avoid materializing a (B,H,Tq,Tk,N,8) tensor. This makes octonion training orders of magnitude slower than peers and poisons every fixed-FLOPs/wall-clock comparison it appears in (budget spent in Python overhead, not math). It also makes octonion infeasible for the EP-E scaling ladder.

WHAT: replace the loop with CHUNKED vectorization: process Tq in tiles of size c, computing the non-associative product with batched einsum over the tile. Choose c automatically from a memory budget: B*H*c*Tk*N*8*dtype_size <= budget (expose --octonion-tile-budget or derive from available memory; document default). CRITICAL: octonion multiplication is NON-ASSOCIATIVE - the exact parenthesization ((Q*K_conj)*V) must be preserved; any "optimization" that reorders the product changes the mathematics.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Outputs match the current loop implementation on seeded inputs to atol<=1e-6 fp32 (target bitwise where reduction order is unchanged) across shapes: Tq in {1, 7, 256}, GQA on/off, B in {1, 4}.
- >=5x wall-clock improvement on CPU at T=256 (record before/after numbers in the close notes; store microbench JSON under artifacts/perf/ following the pattern of existing bead model_guided_research-2cg per-op microbench).
- Property test added to the B2 suite asserting alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y and norm multiplicativity |xy|=|x||y| hold identically for the vectorized path (guards against silent reordering).
- Decode path (Tq==1) remains fast - no regression.

PITFALLS: omul builds octonions from 2x2 quaternion blocks via Cayley-Dickson (o1*o2 = (ac - d_conj*b, d*a + b*c_conj)) - keep that structure; einsum over blades must reproduce the same multiplication table (verify against a brute-force table test).

DEPENDS ON: A1 (vectorize once, against the final scaffold interface).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- INIT LOGGING: log the auto-chosen tile size and the estimated peak intermediate memory once at module init (debug level) - the tile heuristic must be auditable from logs.
- E2E MICROBENCH SCRIPT: before/after wall-clock + peak-mem JSON artifact under artifacts/perf/ including env info (device, dtype, torch version); rich comparison table printed; this artifact is the bead's evidence of the >=5x claim.
- UNIT TESTS: tile-boundary correctness (Tq not divisible by tile size; Tq smaller than one tile; Tq==1 decode), brute-force multiplication-table equivalence test for the einsum path, OOM-guard behavior when the budget forces tile=1 (must degrade to current per-query behavior, not crash).

title: Tropical max-plus Triton kernel (first custom kernel)
description:
WHY: README Open Question #4: "Hardware: custom kernels for exotic operations?" Tropical attention is the best first target: max-plus GEMM (tropical_max_plus_attention, tropical_attention_torch.py:19-85) is structurally identical to matmul with (+,*) replaced by (max,+), so a Triton tile kernel is a direct transcription of the classic matmul tiling - and tropical is among the most promising mechanisms (1-Lipschitz robustness certificates). The current implementation is memory-bound through broadcast intermediates.

WHAT:
1. Forward Triton kernel: score(q,k) = max_d(q_d + k_d) fused with causal masking, then y_d = max_k(score + v_kd).
2. Custom backward: the subgradient routes through argmax indices - store argmax_d (scores) and argmax_k (aggregation) per output during forward (this mirrors exactly how route extraction works in the JAX demo tropical_geometry_and_idempotent_algebra.py; the kernel byproduct gives us per-sample discrete routes at production scale for free - note this for interpretability follow-ups).
3. Integration behind --tropical-kernel triton|reference with automatic fallback when Triton/CUDA is unavailable (CPU CI must pass untouched).
4. Margin statistics (tropical_gamma_*) computed as a kernel byproduct (runner-up tracking) so the diagnostics don't disappear in fast mode.

TIES POLICY: multiple argmax => deterministic first-index selection in BOTH kernel and reference; assert agreement in tests (otherwise gradients differ silently).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Parity vs reference: max/+ are exact ops, so same-dtype outputs must match bitwise on tie-free inputs; tie-handling test passes; gradcheck (fp64 reference path) on small shapes.
- Speedup >=3x at T>=1024 on GPU, recorded in artifacts/perf/.
- B1 tropical certificates (1-Lipschitz, gauge-fix invariance, margin consistency) pass with the kernel enabled.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (stable integration surface) and existing bead model_guided_research-c6h (Triton hotspot benchmarking plan - confirms tropical is the top-value target and sets measurement methodology BEFORE we invest in kernel work).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- PATH LOGGING: log the active kernel path (triton|reference) and the REASON (flag, no CUDA, no triton import) exactly once at model build - benchmark results must never be ambiguous about which path ran; record the path in summary.json.
- CI: GPU-optional e2e training smoke (--tropical-kernel triton) gated on torch.cuda.is_available + triton import; CPU CI runs the reference path untouched. Gradcheck and tie-policy tests run on CPU against the reference implementation regardless.
- UNIT TESTS: forward bitwise parity on tie-free inputs; deterministic first-index tie test (constructed ties); backward parity vs reference autograd; margin-stat byproduct equals reference computation.

THEORY-PROGRAM ADDENDUM (see 8gk.1): the kernel should support FINITE-BETA mode (log-sum-exp semiring aggregation, numerically-stable streaming LSE per tile - same tiling, different reduction op) so dequantization-annealing runs (beta schedules from 8gk.1 / lab.3) get kernel speed throughout training, not only at the tropical endpoint. The beta=inf path must remain bitwise-identical to the max-plus path; finite-beta parity tests against the reference LSE implementation with magnitude-scaled tolerances. If finite-beta complicates v1, ship max-plus first and file the finite-beta extension as a discovered-from bead - do not silently drop it.

title: EPIC THEORY-I: Non-archimedean unification - attention over valued fields
description:
THE DISCOVERY: five of this project's frameworks are not parallel experiments - they are facets of ONE mathematical object, geometry over valued fields (fields equipped with a valuation v satisfying v(xy)=v(x)+v(y) and the ultrametric inequality v(x+y) >= min(v(x),v(y))):

1. ULTRAMETRIC/p-ADIC: the mechanism's similarity LCP(x,y) IS the p-adic valuation v_p(x-y) of the difference - longest common prefix of base-p digits is literally how valuation is computed. The trie in ultrametric_attention_torch.py is a finite subtree of the Bruhat-Tits tree.
2. TROPICAL: (min,+)/(max,+) arithmetic IS the image of valued-field arithmetic under v: products map to sums exactly, sums map to min generically. Tropical attention computes the valuation shadow of p-adic bilinear attention.
3. SOFTMAX ITSELF: log-sum-exp is Maslov quantization - for every beta, x (+)_beta y = (1/beta)log(e^(beta x)+e^(beta y)) is an EXACT semiring, deforming continuously to (max,+) at beta=infinity. Standard smooth attention and tropical attention are two points on one semiring family; the codebase already contains both endpoints without knowing they are connected.
4. IFS/FRACTAL: the address space of an IFS carries the cylinder ultrametric d(a,b) = r^LCP(a,b); FractalKV and the ultrametric trie are the same data structure with different radii conventions; capacity is governed by the Moran equation.
5. SURREAL/TRANSSERIES: Conway normal form is a Hahn series representation; dominance order IS valuation by leading term; the surreal demo's dominant-balance probing is tropicalization over a series field.
6. HYPERBOLIC (#13): tree boundaries carry ultrametrics via d(xi,eta) = exp(-(xi|eta)) where (.|.) is the Gromov product - which in a tree EQUALS LCP depth. Hyperbolic attention degenerates to ultrametric attention in the rescaled curvature -> -infinity limit.

LEAPFROG THESIS: conventional deep learning fixes one semiring ((+,x) with softmax glue) and bolts on hierarchy, sparsity, robustness, and quantization as engineering afterthoughts. Treating the SEMIRING AS A DESIGN AXIS and the valuation as the organizing geometry yields: exact robustness certificates (the tropical limit), native hierarchy (the valuation tree), principled quantization with non-accumulating error (digit truncation in the ultrametric - the strong triangle inequality forbids error accumulation), and a smooth, certified training path connecting all of it (Maslov dequantization annealing).

CHILDREN: T1.1 Maslov dequantization annealing (the new training algorithm); T1.2 the valuation dictionary theorems + valued-attention reference mechanism; T1.3 Newton-polytope capacity theory; T1.4 p-adic precision as a quantization paradigm; T1.5 IFS=ultrametric unification + Moran capacity; T1.6 hyperbolic-boundary correspondence (Gromov product = LCP).

EVIDENCE DISCIPLINE: every child preregisters its predictions in the G1 hypothesis registry before benchmarking and ships numerical theorem-verification tests (exact where the math is exact, tolerance-bounded where floating point intrudes).
notes: POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass incorporated): the unification claim must be packaged as a DEFINED FORMAL OBJECT, not as rhetoric. Replace 'five frameworks are one object' with the defensible-and-still-bold version: these mechanisms are all REPRESENTATIONS OF A VALUATION GEOMETRY - tokens/states are mapped into objects carrying a dominance valuation; attention is comparison of leading terms; routing is movement between valuation cells; hierarchy is the induced ultrametric/tree boundary. The definition itself (axioms of a 'valued attention structure' and the verification that each mechanism is an instance) is a first-class deliverable of 8gk.2. Referee consensus: Pillar I is the most immediately paperable piece of the entire program - children 8gk.1/8gk.2 should be sequenced first among theory work.

title: Maslov dequantization annealing: softmax-to-tropical as an exact semiring homotopy
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: For beta > 0 define x (+)_beta y = (1/beta) log(e^(beta x) + e^(beta y)). For EVERY finite beta this is a commutative, associative semiring operation on the reals, with + as multiplication, isomorphic to ordinary (R+, +, x) via x -> e^(beta x) (Maslov quantization). As beta -> infinity it converges uniformly to max, with explicit rate: max(x_i) <= ((+)_beta)_i x_i <= max(x_i) + log(n)/beta for n-fold combinations. Define LOG-DOMAIN ATTENTION entirely inside this semiring: out_d = [((+)_beta)_i (s_i + v_id)] - [((+)_beta)_i s_i] (the subtraction is semiring division = normalization). TWO ENDPOINT FACTS: (1) at beta -> infinity this converges EXACTLY to the codebase's tropical attention WITH score centering - tropical_score_center in tropical_attention_torch.py is the beta=infinity shadow of softmax normalization; the code already contains both endpoints of the family without knowing they are connected. (2) The whole family is 1-Lipschitz in sup-norm for every beta (LSE and max are both nonexpansive), so the tropical robustness certificate extends to FINITE beta: ROUTE-STABILITY LEMMA - if the runner-up margin gamma (already measured by tropical_record_margins) satisfies gamma > log(n)/beta, then beta-attention selects the same argmax route as tropical attention, and inherits its certificate. Proof is ~5 lines from the LSE-max sandwich; write it out in the theory note.

THE NEW TRAINING ALGORITHM - DEQUANTIZATION ANNEALING: train with a schedule beta: 1 -> infinity (a continuation/homotopy method with semiring semantics). Early training gets smooth gradients everywhere; the late-time limit gives certified piecewise-linear routing. The discrete limit is not a straight-through hack or an RL workaround - it is the EXACT algebraic limit of the smooth family, with a convergence rate and a certificate. This dissolves the soft-attention-vs-hard-routing dichotomy that the entire MoE/retrieval/discrete-decoding literature works around heuristically.

WHY LEAPFROG: every existing approach to hard routing (Gumbel-softmax, straight-through estimators, REINFORCE) approximates gradients of a discrete object. Here the discrete object is the limit of exact semirings, the interpolation parameter is one scalar with algebraic meaning, route stability is certifiable DURING training (watch gamma vs log(n)/beta), and the endpoint model is 1-Lipschitz-certified by construction.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE (markdown_documentation/, house style): the semiring family and its axioms; the two endpoint theorems; the route-stability lemma with proof; convergence rates; relation of beta to softmax temperature (beta = 1/tau, but the LOG-DOMAIN aggregation differs from linear-domain softmax averaging - state the precise relationship and why the log-domain form is the certificate-carrying one).
2. IMPLEMENTATION: --semiring-beta flag (float or schedule spec) on the tropical attention path in nanochat; beta=inf reproduces current tropical attention bitwise; beta finite uses the LSE form. Schedule support: linear, exponential, and ordinal-orchestrated (T3.3 client).
3. EXPERIMENTS (preregister in G1 first): anneal schedules vs fixed-beta vs POST-HOC SNAP (train soft, discretize at the end - the baseline everyone implicitly uses). Measure: loss retention of the annealed-to-tropical model vs its beta=1 starting point at matched budget (predict >= 95% retention); certificate coverage = fraction of eval tokens with nonvacuous route-stability margins (predict annealed >> post-hoc snap); robustness under eps-bounded perturbations (tie to the C3 tropical robustness prediction).
4. METRICS: log beta and certificate coverage per log_interval into metrics.jsonl (D2 schema gains two fields).

TESTS & LOGGING (numerical theorem checks - the math is the spec):
- Semiring axiom property tests at random beta: associativity, commutativity, distributivity of + over (+)_beta, to fp tolerance scaled by magnitude.
- Endpoint convergence test: |LSE_beta - max| <= log(n)/beta asserted across random inputs and n (this inequality is EXACT - any violation is an implementation bug).
- Route-stability lemma verified empirically: construct inputs with gamma > log(n)/beta, assert argmax routes identical to tropical; construct gamma below threshold, observe (and log) route divergence is possible.
- beta=inf code path bitwise-equals the existing tropical implementation (regression-protect the endpoint).
- Training-loop e2e smoke: 200-step CPU run with a beta schedule; metrics stream contains monotone beta and coverage fields; rich live logging shows beta, gamma distribution, coverage.

RELATION: tropical_attention_torch.py (both endpoints live there); A7 Triton kernel (note added: kernel should support finite-beta mode); T3.3 (ordinal orchestration of the schedule is its first client); G1/C3 (preregistration); C4 (the annealed variant joins the scorecard as its own row).
notes:
POLISH ROUND 2 - MATHEMATICAL PRECISION FIX (apply before implementation): the route-stability lemma as phrased in the description conflates two separate facts. The correct certificate structure has THREE parts, each with different conditions:
(a) OUTPUT PROXIMITY (unconditional): |out_beta - out_inf| <= 2 log(n)/beta for every input - both LSE terms are within log(n)/beta of their max counterparts. No margin condition needed.
(b) MASS CONCENTRATION (the gamma condition's real content): the softmax weight on the top-scoring key satisfies w_max >= 1/(1 + (n-1) e^(-beta gamma)) >= 1 - n e^(-beta gamma). So the clean statement is: ROUTE MASS >= 1 - eps ONCE gamma >= log(n/eps)/beta. Note the score argmax itself is BETA-INDEPENDENT (softmax is monotone; the largest score always gets the largest weight) - do not write code that 'checks whether the argmax route matches tropical'; it always does. What changes with beta is how much output mass rides the dominant route.
(c) PERTURBATION ROBUSTNESS OF THE ROUTE (beta-independent): the argmax route is stable under sup-norm input perturbations smaller than gamma/2 - this is the tropical certificate and it holds at every beta.
Certificate coverage during annealing should therefore report: fraction of tokens with mass-concentration >= 1-eps (tracks beta progress) AND fraction with perturbation margin >= delta (tracks robustness). Two curves, not one. The theory note must state the lemma in this three-part form with the one-line proofs.

E2E CONTRACT: a dequantization-annealing mini-run (tiny config, CPU, ~200 steps, beta schedule to a large finite value + final snap) joins the rz8.8 e2e pipeline script as part of this bead's acceptance (Scenario 5 - see rz8.8 notes); the run must log both certificate-coverage curves and assert the endpoint model's tropical-mode parity.

POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass):
1. WORDING SHARPENING (adopt in the theory note and all claims): 'certified 1-Lipschitz combinatorial routing' is imprecise - the max VALUE map is 1-Lipschitz, but the argmax ROUTE map is globally discontinuous (it jumps at tropical hypersurfaces). The sharper, correct claim: PIECEWISE-LINEAR 1-LIPSCHITZ SCORE DYNAMICS plus MARGIN-CERTIFIED LOCALLY CONSTANT ROUTES. This is stronger, not weaker - it says exactly where the discontinuities live (the corner locus) and certifies constancy away from them.
2. TIGHT CONSTANT: the mass-concentration bound is p_top >= 1/(1 + (n-1) e^(-beta gamma)), so the eps-form threshold is gamma >= (1/beta) log((n-1)/eps) - use (n-1), not n, in the theory note and the certificate code (matching the round-2 note's structure, with the tight constant).
3. POSITIONING REQUIREMENT (the theory note must engage, not discover-in-review): Litvinov's Maslov-dequantization exposition (the exact h log(e^(u/h)+e^(v/h)) -> max deformation - our semiring family IS this picture applied to attention); the recent Tropical Attention literature (max-plus attention with approximation results for tropical dynamic-programming circuits) - state precisely what is NEW here relative to it: the exact finite-beta semiring HOMOTOPY as a training algorithm, the three-part route certificate, the annealing-vs-post-hoc-snap protocol, and the certificate-coverage observable. If the positioning cannot articulate a delta, the bead's scope shrinks honestly to 'independent rediscovery plus the annealing protocol' - say so rather than overclaim.

title: The valuation dictionary: p-adic = tropical = dominance, and the valued-attention reference mechanism
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: make precise, prove, and numerically verify the dictionary that unifies three frameworks:
(a) LCP IS VALUATION: for x, y in Z_p with base-p digit expansions, LCP(x,y) = v_p(x - y) exactly. The ultrametric mechanism's similarity is the valuation of a difference; its trie is a finite subtree of the Bruhat-Tits tree for PGL_2(Q_p).
(b) TROPICAL IS THE VALUATION SHADOW: v is a homomorphism from (Q_p, x) to (Z, +), and v(x + y) >= min(v(x), v(y)) with EQUALITY whenever v(x) != v(y) (no leading-term cancellation). Hence the TROPICALIZATION THEOREM FOR ATTENTION: for valuation-generic inputs, v(<q, k>_{Q_p}) = (min,+) inner product of the valuation vectors - TROPICAL ATTENTION COMPUTES EXACTLY THE VALUATION OF p-ADIC BILINEAR ATTENTION. The tie/cancellation locus (where the inequality is strict) is the tropical variety itself - the 'corner locus' where routes switch; this is not a defect but the geometry of decision boundaries.
(c) DOMINANCE IS VALUATION: over Puiseux/Hahn series fields (and the surreal numbers via Conway normal form, which IS a Hahn series representation), the valuation is leading-exponent extraction = the dominance order that the surreal demo probes. The surreal framework's dominant-balance machinery is tropicalization over a series field. One backbone - digits (Q_p), piecewise-linear geometry (tropical), asymptotics (Hahn/transseries) - three shadows.

THE MECHANISM - VALUED ATTENTION (reference design): keys/queries as finite-precision elements of Q_p (length-K digit vectors with genuine valuation arithmetic); scores = alpha^(v_p(<q,k> - c)) or -v_p(...) directly. Show the existing ultrametric mechanism (digit-similarity only) and tropical mechanism (valuation-arithmetic only) are its two projections. THE ALGORITHMIC PAYOFF: the Bruhat-Tits/Berkovich tree is the native data structure; nearest-ball and ball-intersection queries on the tree give EXACT sub-quadratic attention algorithms with correctness proofs in the ultrametric - directly answering the expected E3 finding that the current kernel-mode torch port is quadratic-with-better-constants. A ball-tree attention whose correctness is a theorem, not an approximation bound.

WHY LEAPFROG: hierarchical attention today is heuristic (LSH buckets, learned clustering, block sparsity) with approximation-and-pray guarantees. This gives hierarchy a SEMANTICS - the quantity computed is a valuation - and algorithms inherit exactness from the ultrametric. It also explains a mystery: WHY tropical structure appears wherever hierarchy does (it is the valuation image - they are the same thing seen twice).

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE with the three correspondence statements and proofs (the ingredients are classical; the assembly and the mechanism-level consequences are the contribution; the tropicalization-of-attention statement with its genericity condition deserves a careful proof including the measure of the cancellation locus under digit distributions).
2. NUMERICAL VERIFICATION SUITE: exact integer arithmetic, no tolerances - v(xy) = v(x)+v(y) always; v(x+y) = min generically with strictness exactly on constructed cancellation cases; LCP-equals-valuation on random digit vectors; trie scores vs valuation formula agreement (hard-digit mode must agree EXACTLY).
3. VALUED-ATTENTION reference implementation at demo level - extend the ultrametric JAX demo IN PLACE (per AGENTS.md) with a valued-attention section + rich side-by-side table showing one attention computation rendered in all three shadows (digits / tropical numbers / leading exponents).
4. BALL-TREE SUB-QUADRATIC PROTOTYPE: tree-based exact attention for hard-digit valued attention; complexity analysis; benchmark hook for E3 (this is the concrete content of E3's anticipated discovered-from bead 'sub-quadratic ultrametric training path' - coordinate).
5. REGISTRY PREDICTIONS: ball-tree valued attention matches kernel-mode quality on hierarchical retrieval (C1 task 3) at >= 4x wall-clock advantage at T=4096; tie-locus density (fraction of cancellation events) is small and DECREASES under training (the model learns to avoid its own decision boundaries - measurable, novel observable).

TESTS & LOGGING: all dictionary identities as exact integer property tests (Hypothesis strategies over digit vectors, p in {2,3,5}); cancellation-locus tests constructed adversarially; cross-implementation agreement tests (trie vs formula vs tropical shadow); demo emits the three-shadow table with rich formatting; ball-tree prototype validated against brute-force attention exactly (hard digits) on randomized cases with seeds logged.

RELATION: ultrametric_worlds demo + ultrametric_attention_torch.py (the digit machinery); tropical demo (route extraction = corner locus); surreal demo (dominance engine); E3 (the sub-quadratic question); T1.4 (depends on this dictionary); T3.2 (the Hahn-series shadow feeds resurgent fitting).
notes: POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass) - NEW FIRST-CLASS DELIVERABLE: the theory note must open by DEFINING the common formal object, then prove the instances. Working definition to refine in-bead: a VALUED ATTENTION STRUCTURE is (V, Gamma, v, sim) where V is the representation space, Gamma a totally ordered value group, v: V x V -> Gamma a dominance valuation (ultrametric-inducing), and sim a monotone transform of v used for scoring; required axioms: the strong triangle property for v, monotonicity of sim, and compatibility of v with whatever algebraic operations the mechanism uses (the almost-homomorphism property). THEN the instance proofs: Q_p digits (Gamma = Z), tropical (Gamma = R, v = leading exponent), Hahn/surreal (Gamma = the exponent group, v = dominance), IFS cylinders (Gamma = R via radius products), tree/hyperbolic boundary (v = Gromov product). Each instance proof is short; their assembly is the theorem-shaped version of the unification, and it is what makes the epic's claim defensible in review rather than rhetorical. The capstone paper's Pillar I section opens with this definition.

title: Newton-polytope capacity theory for tropical attention
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: a tropical polynomial f(x) = max_a (c_a + <a, x>) is piecewise linear; its linear regions are the cells of the regular subdivision of its Newton polytope New(f) = conv{a} induced by the lifting c_a; the number of regions is bounded by the number of vertices of the upper hull, hence by lattice-point counts. Known result (Zhang-Naitzat-Lim 2018): ReLU networks are tropical rational maps, and region-counting via polytopes recovers expressivity bounds (Montufar et al). WHAT IS NEW HERE: nobody has computed the polytope geometry of tropical ATTENTION - a composition of TWO max-plus bilinear stages (scores = max over d of q_d + k_d; outputs = max over keys of score + v). The key structural feature: the inner max ranges over KEYS - a DATA-DEPENDENT index set of size T - so regions multiply combinatorially with context length, unlike feedforward tropical layers whose monomial sets are fixed by the architecture. Derive: (1) the Newton polytope of the single-head tropical attention map (q, K, V) -> out as a function of (T, d); (2) region-count upper bounds via upper-hull vertex counts; (3) the MULTI-HEAD superposition law - regions of a sum of tropical maps are counted by MIXED subdivisions, i.e. Bernstein-style mixed-volume bounds: head superposition is mixed-volume accumulation, giving the first algebraic account of what adding heads buys in capacity terms.

CONJECTURE (falsifiable, countable): per-parameter linear-region count of tropical attention exceeds that of parameter-matched ReLU MLPs by a factor growing with T. If true, tropical attention is provably more region-efficient per parameter in long contexts - a constructive expressivity separation, not a circuit-class upper bound.

DESIGN COROLLARY - POLYHEDRAL ARCHITECTURE DESIGN: region capacity is maximized, for a fixed number of monomials, by point configurations in general position whose upper hulls have many vertices (cyclic-polytope-type configurations are extremal by the Upper Bound Theorem). Translation: choose the SUPPORT PATTERN of the score/value maps (which index sets appear in each max) as a designed combinatorial structure rather than dense default - architecture design by polyhedral combinatorics. Prototype: structured-support tropical attention vs dense at equal parameter count.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the attention-map polytope computation; region bounds with proofs; the mixed-volume multi-head law; the conjecture stated with its precise counting protocol.
2. EMPIRICAL REGION COUNTING at small scale: enumerate realized activation patterns (argmax routes - the tropical demo's route extraction machinery does exactly this; reuse it) of trained tropical nanochat models over large input samples; growth curves of realized regions vs T and vs parameters; compare to the theoretical bound (must respect it ALWAYS) and to parameter-matched ReLU baselines (the conjecture).
3. STRUCTURED-SUPPORT EXPERIMENT: cyclic-type support patterns vs dense, equal params, on >= 1 diagnostic task + LM smoke; preregister the prediction (structured >= dense).
4. REGISTRY: the conjecture and the design-corollary prediction, with counting protocols as the metric definitions.

TESTS & LOGGING: route-enumeration harness, deterministic seeds, exact counting (routes are discrete - no tolerances); BOUND-RESPECT ASSERTION: measured count <= theoretical bound in every configuration - a single violation falsifies the derivation and must fail the suite loudly (this is the strongest kind of test: the theory is the oracle); rich tables of measured-vs-bound curves; fixture-based regression on small exactly-solvable cases (T=2, d=2 where regions can be enumerated by hand and MUST match exactly).

RELATION: tropical demo route tracking; T1.1 (the finite-beta family smooths the same polytopes - region counts at finite beta interpolate; optional extension); C4 (capacity claims become scorecard predictions); existing bead qq7 (ablations) shares harness patterns.

title: p-adic precision: quantization with non-accumulating error and Hensel-lift curricula
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: in p-adic arithmetic, truncating to k digits is metrically exact and STRUCTURALLY STABLE in a way float rounding is not. THE FLAT-ERROR LEMMA (3-line proof from the strong triangle inequality): if errors e_1, ..., e_N each have v_p(e_i) >= k (i.e., live below digit k), then v_p(sum e_i) >= k and v_p(prod (1+e_i) - 1) >= k - truncation error CANNOT ACCUMULATE under sums: N rounding errors of size p^-k sum to an error still of size p^-k, versus sqrt(N) growth (or worse) in archimedean floating point. Quantization error in the ultrametric world is FLAT in depth and width.
CONSEQUENCE - QUANTIZATION AS VALUATION TRUNCATION: for network components whose arithmetic is p-adic-native (ultrametric digit machinery, IFS addresses, integer-coefficient tropical scores), low-precision inference = digit truncation with a THEOREM about error non-accumulation, replacing per-tensor empirical heuristics. The honest boundary: the lemma governs the p-adic-native subcomputations; error leaks at the interface to archimedean parts (the float-valued V aggregation). Characterizing that interface precisely - which quantities cross the valuation boundary, and what error they carry - is a core deliverable, not a footnote.
HENSEL LIFTING AS CURRICULUM: solving f(x) = 0 mod p, then lifting the solution to mod p^2, p^3, ... is the p-adic Newton method, and each lift PRESERVES the previous residue exactly. Training translation: learn digit-0 structure first (a p-way quantized model), then refine digit-by-digit, with the guarantee that refinement never destroys coarse structure (the lift fixes the residue). Float curricula have no such invariant - early learning is routinely overwritten. Prediction: digit-wise curricula show monotone coarse-metric improvement (the digit-0 model's decisions are preserved as digits are added), trainable-vs-end-to-end at equal budget.
MAHLER BASIS: continuous functions Z_p -> Q_p have canonical expansions f = sum a_n * binomial(x, n) with a_n -> 0; truncation is canonical compression with certified ultrametric error. Use as the function class for heads operating on hierarchical addresses (the trie addresses ARE elements of Z_p) - a principled replacement for MLPs on tree positions.

WHY LEAPFROG: post-training quantization is the most economically important deployment problem in the field and is handled by calibration heuristics with empirical cliffs. A mechanism class whose precision axis carries theorems (flat error, residue preservation, canonical truncation) is a new paradigm even if it only governs the hierarchical components - and our scorecard can measure exactly how much of the network can live on the p-adic side.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: flat-error lemma + proof; the archimedean-interface analysis (where valuation guarantees end); Hensel-curriculum invariant statement; Mahler-basis head design.
2. IMPLEMENT digit-truncation inference for the ultrametric mechanism (--ultrametric-digits-k at eval time): quality-vs-k curves on C1 hierarchical tasks + LM loss, SAME checkpoints evaluated at multiple k, against float-quantization quality-vs-bits curves for the standard baseline (the comparison everyone understands).
3. HENSEL CURRICULUM PROTOTYPE on a small hierarchical task: train digit-0 model, lift (freeze residue, add digit-1 parameters), compare vs end-to-end; verify the residue-preservation invariant EXACTLY at each stage (the lifted model reduced mod p^j must reproduce the stage-j model's discrete decisions).
4. REGISTRY PREDICTIONS: (a) digit-truncation curves are FLATTER than float-quantization curves (graceful vs cliff degradation - quantify as area-under-quality-curve); (b) Hensel curriculum matches end-to-end final quality at equal budget with monotone digit-wise improvement; (c) the flat-error lemma's prediction visible end-to-end: quality at digit-k truncation independent of model DEPTH for the p-adic-native path (depth-independence is the experimentally striking signature).

TESTS & LOGGING: exact valuation-bookkeeping tests (integer arithmetic, no tolerances) for the lemma under sums and products including adversarial cancellation cases; truncation-inference parity harness producing per-k rich curves with logged seeds/checkpoints; curriculum stage logs asserting exact residue preservation (a single violated residue fails the run loudly); interface-leak instrumentation: log the valuation of every quantity crossing the p-adic/float boundary so the leak budget is empirical, visible, and regression-tracked.

DEPENDS ON: T1.2 (the valuation dictionary and valued-attention reference supply the semantics and the digit machinery contract).

title: IFS = weighted ultrametric: unify fractal memory with the trie, capacity by Moran dimension
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the address space {1..m}^N of an iterated function system with contraction ratios r_i carries the cylinder metric d(a,b) = r^(LCP(a,b)) (uniform ratios) - an ULTRAMETRIC. With non-uniform ratios, d(a,b) = prod of r_(a_j) over the common prefix j <= LCP: a WEIGHTED ultrametric. PROPOSITION 1 (equivalence): FractalKV (iterated_function_systems demo, fractal_attention_torch.py routing) and the ultrametric trie are the SAME data structure - a valuation tree - differing only in radii conventions: trie = uniform r = 1/p; fractal = learned/structured radii. Non-uniform learned radii strictly generalize BOTH mechanisms. PROPOSITION 2 (capacity): the similarity dimension s solves the Moran equation sum_i r_i^s = 1 (for uniform ratios s = log m / log(1/r)); the number of eps-separated addresses scales as eps^(-s); hence MEMORY CAPACITY under a readout-noise floor eps is eps^(-s), with collision probability of random writes computable in closed form from cylinder measures. PROPOSITION 3 (zero interference): writes to disjoint cylinders are EXACTLY orthogonal in the ultrametric geometry - interference is zero, not small; the demo's catastrophic-forgetting benchmark results are a theorem, not an empirical tendency, whenever the addressing respects cylinder disjointness.
OPTIMAL MEMORY DESIGN: maximize capacity eps^(-s) subject to a Lipschitz/readout budget by choosing (m, r_i, depth) - a constrained optimization with closed-form structure via Lagrange multipliers on the Moran constraint. Imbalanced data wants imbalanced radii: branches carrying more probability mass should contract LESS (get more resolution); the optimal r_i profile is computable from the address distribution (mirror of Huffman/arithmetic coding, but for geometric memory - make the coding-theory analogy precise in the note: the Moran exponent plays the role of entropy).

WHY LEAPFROG: external-memory designs (kNN-LM, memory layers, product-key memories) tune capacity and collision behavior empirically. This gives capacity, interference, and distortion as THEOREMS with design knobs, unifies two of our mechanisms into one parameterized family (halving maintenance, doubling the evidence base per experiment), and imports a coding-theory design principle (radius allocation = bit allocation) nobody applies to memory geometry.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the three propositions with proofs; the optimal-radius derivation; the coding-theory dictionary.
2. UNIFICATION PLAN (plan, not execution): document how fractal_attention_torch.py routing and the ultrametric trie share the address structure; actual code merge is gated on certify (B1) + goldens and gets its own discovered-from bead when this note is accepted - respect the in-place/no-churn rules.
3. EXPERIMENTS: (a) capacity scaling: vary (m, r, depth) in the FractalKV demo, measure retrieval-accuracy cliff location, compare against the eps^(-s) prediction (the cliff should move EXACTLY as Moran predicts - strong quantitative test); (b) learned per-branch radii on imbalanced hierarchical data vs uniform radii (predict: learned wins, and the learned profile correlates with branch mass per the optimal-allocation formula - check the correlation, not just the win); (c) zero-interference verification at the implementation level.
4. REGISTRY PREDICTIONS: cliff-location matches Moran within CI across a 3x3 (m, r) grid; learned radii beat uniform on imbalanced trees by >= preregistered margin; radius-mass correlation > 0.8.

TESTS & LOGGING: Monte Carlo dimension agreement test (box-counting dimension of generated attractors vs Moran formula within CI - validates the geometry layer); EXACT zero-interference test (disjoint-cylinder writes leave unrelated reads bitwise unchanged in the trie/KV implementation - any nonzero diff is a bug, no tolerance); capacity-cliff harness with rich curves (accuracy vs load, per configuration, predicted cliff overlaid); learned-radii training logs include the radius profile per epoch so its convergence toward the optimal allocation is visible in the metrics stream.

RELATION: iterated_function_systems demo (FractalKV + forgetting benchmark); ultrametric trie; T1.2 (both are valuation trees - this bead is the dictionary's fourth column); C1 task 3 (hierarchical retrieval is the natural testbed).
notes:
POLISH ROUND 2 - RIGOR CAVEAT: the Moran-equation dimension and the eps^(-s) capacity law hold under the OPEN SET CONDITION (contraction images sufficiently separated). For LEARNED radii/maps, overlap is possible, and then the Moran s is an UPPER BOUND on the similarity dimension rather than the exact value. The theory note must state this, and the experiments must measure overlap (collision rate between sibling cylinders) alongside capacity so cliff-location deviations can be attributed correctly: deviation WITH overlap = expected (bound not tight); deviation WITHOUT overlap = theory problem (the interesting case - investigate before publishing). The trie/hard-digit instantiation satisfies OSC automatically (disjoint prefixes) - state that too: the zero-interference theorem is unconditional for the trie, OSC-conditional for general learned IFS.
ALSO: the weighted-ultrametric claim (non-uniform radii d(a,b) = product of r over the common prefix) needs its ultrametric-inequality proof written out in the note (it is true - the products agree along shared prefixes and shrink with depth - but the three-line argument should be explicit, not asserted).

title: Gromov product = LCP: hyperbolic attention as the continuous completion of ultrametric attention
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the Gromov product (x|y)_o = (d(x,o) + d(y,o) - d(x,y)) / 2 measures how long geodesics from o to x and from o to y travel together. PROPOSITION 1 (exact): in a rooted metric tree, (x|y)_root = depth of the deepest common ancestor of x and y - i.e., EXACTLY the LCP depth of their address strings. Tree boundaries carry the ultrametric d(xi, eta) = exp(-(xi|eta)); therefore ultrametric attention scores alpha^LCP(q,k) = exp(-(log 1/alpha) * (q|k)) are GROMOV-PRODUCT KERNELS - the ultrametric mechanism is boundary-of-a-tree attention, exactly. PROPOSITION 2 (the correction terms ARE semantics): in hyperbolic space, distance-based scores decompose as -d(q,k) = 2(q|k)_o - d(q,o) - d(k,o): a Gromov-product (hierarchy-overlap) term PLUS radial terms. The radial terms measure depth-in-hierarchy (generality vs specificity of a token's position); current hyperbolic-attention literature conflates the two. DESIGN: score = 2(q|k) - lambda_q |q| - lambda_k |k| with LEARNABLE radial gates lambda - separating 'how much do our hierarchies overlap' from 'how deep/specific are we', each with geometric meaning. PROPOSITION 3 (the homotopy): rescaled hyperbolic spaces converge to R-trees as curvature -> -infinity (delta-hyperbolicity delta -> 0; CAT(kappa) asymptotics), and at c -> 0 hyperbolic attention reduces to Euclidean dot-product attention (the F4 reduction). Hence ONE mechanism with learnable curvature c per head traverses: Euclidean attention (c=0) <-> hyperbolic attention (finite c) <-> tree/ultrametric LCP attention (c -> -infinity, rescaled). The planned three-way comparison (hyperbolic vs ultrametric vs fractal, F4/F6) collapses into a HOMOTOPY FAMILY: train with learnable c and READ OFF, per head and per depth, which geometry language actually wants - an interpretable dial instead of a benchmark bake-off.

WHY LEAPFROG: resolves the hierarchy-bias question empirically per-head rather than per-architecture, with degeneration theorems making the answer interpretable (the learned c VALUE means something; c collapsing to ~0 is the honest 'language is not hierarchical here' verdict and is explicitly welcomed). Also upgrades the F4 design from 'another hyperbolic attention' to the continuous completion of an exact discrete theory - reviewers can check the tree case by hand.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: Propositions 1-3 with proofs at the appropriate rigor (1 is exact and elementary - prove fully; 2 is an identity - derive; 3 cite the metric-geometry literature precisely and state the rescaling convention used); the radial-gating design and its invariances.
2. REQUIREMENTS INTO F4 (note added to mnn.4): scores in Gromov-product form with radial gates; learnable per-head curvature; the c -> -infinity degeneration to LCP as an acceptance test alongside the existing c -> 0 Euclidean reduction.
3. NUMERICAL DEGENERATION SUITE: explicit tree embeddings where (q|k) is computable exactly as an integer (assert Gromov product == LCP depth EXACTLY); curvature sweeps c in {0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100, rescaled} with monotone-convergence assertions toward (a) standard attention outputs and (b) ultrametric attention outputs at the two ends.
4. OBSERVABLE: per-head learned c and per-head mean radial gate values into the D2 metrics stream - the headline interpretability readout (which heads go hierarchical, at which depths, on which data).
5. REGISTRY PREDICTIONS: on hierarchical retrieval (C1 task 3), >= 25% of heads learn |c| above the hierarchy threshold while flat-task controls collapse to ~0; radial gating beats pure-distance scores on depth-sensitive queries by a preregistered margin; the c -> -infinity-trained model matches dedicated ultrametric attention on the same task (the homotopy endpoint reproduces the specialist).

TESTS & LOGGING: exact integer tree-case test (no tolerances - Gromov product on constructed trees IS LCP); degeneration sweeps with per-c convergence curves logged and asserted monotone within tolerance; radial-gate ablation harness; fp32-islands policy inherited from F4 numerics section verified under bf16 autocast; rich per-head c/gate tables in eval reports.

DEPENDS ON: mnn.4 / F4 (the design doc absorbs these requirements - F4 is being raised to P2 accordingly).
RELATION: T1.2 (boundary ultrametric = the valuation picture; this bead closes the geometric side of the dictionary); F5/F6 (implementations inherit the upgraded design); existing ultrametric mechanism (the degeneration target).
